name: Beginner runner training for first 5K
description: Never raced before, minimal running history. Plan should be conservative: build aerobic base with mostly easy running, very little intensity, and walk-run intervals if needed.
must_have: Easy-paced runs make up ≥80% of run volume
At most one quality session per week (and even then, mild — e.g. strides or short tempo segments)
Run volume increases gradually (no jumps >10% week-over-week)
Cross-training or rest filling the non-run days
must_not_have: More than 2 quality sessions in any week
Running 4+ days/week (athlete is new — recovery capacity is low)
Heavy lifting that interferes with the priority sport (running)
Daily running with no rest days
